steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
"While some commentators lauded... less ______ observers countered..." The first group holds an optimistic view (transparency is good).

The second group holds a critical, pessimistic view (blogs exacerbate polarization).

The word "less" indicates we need a positive quality that the critical group lacks.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
The contrast structure: some commentators "lauded this development" with optimism; "less ______ observers countered" with skepticism. The blank needs a word meaning "optimistic" — the critics are LESS optimistic. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — recalcitrant: stubbornly resistant; "less recalcitrant" would mean less resistant, but the critics ARE more resistant. ✗
  • Choice B — earnest: serious / sincere; "less earnest" misses the optimism/pessimism contrast that the passage sets up. ✗
  • Choice C — sanguine: optimistic; "less sanguine observers" = less optimistic critics, matching their skeptical counter. ✓
  • Choice D — misanthropic: hating humanity; "less misanthropic" would mean kinder, but the critics are MORE negative. ✗
That matches Choice C — sanguine.
